rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Implementation-level contracts may only use assign for output features or declared auxiliary variables; guarantees are not allowed in implementation scope.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents or paths in component type contracts; only visible ports and features are allowed.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
AGREE expressions must use only identifiers and types visible in the current AADL component scope.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the input model.
Do not redeclare AADL ports as AGREE variables; they are already visible in legal scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct syntax and valid identifiers.
Component type contracts should relate visible inputs to visible outputs; implementation contracts should bind outputs to expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes; use implementation annexes for such bindings.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or paths in component type annexes; only use visible features.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
